channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Generated release notes credit you for Copilot pull requests
item.description: When you generate release notes for a new release, the generated notes include a list of pull requests merged since the last release. For more information about automatically generating release… The post Generated release notes credit you for Copilot pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Enterprise-ready Claude Desktop with Entra ID, APIM, and Microsoft Foundry (No Backend Required)
item.description: How I put corporate sign-in in front of Claude Desktop without writing a single line of backend code. TL;DR — In this post, I show how to securely enable Claude Desktop in enterprise environments using Microsoft Entra ID, Azure API Management, and Microsoft Foundry — without deploying a custom...
item.dc:creator: LZhang

item.title: From package to postinstall payload: Inside the Mastra npm supply chain compromise
item.description: A poisoned npm package infected 140+ projects with a hidden payload. This report highlights how to detect, hunt, and defend against supply chain attacks using Microsoft Defender and actionable threat intelligence. The post From package to postinstall payload: Inside the Mastra npm supply chain...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Memory-Optimized Table Variables: Performance Under the Microscope
item.description: Introduction Memory-optimized table variables (MOTVs) have been available since SQL Server 2014, but many workloads still use regular on-disk table variables. The common assumption is that MOTVs are "a bit faster" - but how much faster, and under what conditions? When does the difference justify...
item.dc:creator: David_Lyth

item.title: Crypto Clipper uses Tor and worm-like propagation for persistence and control
item.description: Microsoft Threat Intelligence analyzed a cryptocurrency clipper campaign that combines clipboard theft, wallet replacement, Tor-based communications, and worm-like propagation. Beyond stealing cryptocurrency transactions, the malware establishes persistent access and enables follow-on activity...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team and Microsoft Defender Experts

item.title: Scaling High-Performance CAD on Azure Virtual Desktop with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000
item.description: Overview of Siemens NX Multi-Session on Azure This validation study evaluates how Siemens NX 2506 performs in a multi-user AVD environment, using an RTX-based Azure VM. The focus was to test: 15 and 30 concurrent users Single-host vs multi-host configurations Consistent workloads under identical...
item.dc:creator: Sunita_AZ0708

item.title: Read remote repository content with GitHub CLI
item.description: Explore repository content from your terminal without cloning by using two new commands: gh repo read-file and gh repo read-dir. What you can do Use gh repo read-file to read… The post Read remote repository content with GitHub CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Release 2026-05-29
item.description: Release Notes - 2026-05-29 Monitor the release status by regions at AKS-Release-Tracker. Vulnerabilities addressed by AKS releases can be tracked at CVE API viewer. Announcements of upcoming changes and retirements Revision asm-1-27 of the Istio-based service mesh add-on has been deprecated. Please...
item.dc:creator: dyu1208

item.title: Getting more from each token: How Copilot improves context handling and model routing
item.description: How GitHub Copilot is making more of each session go toward useful work, so your credits go further. The post Getting more from each token: How Copilot improves context handling and model routing appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Beyond the benchmark: Advancing security at AI speed
item.description: Read how Microsoft Security has advanced its agentic vulnerability detection system, codename MDASH, integrating into real-world workflows across Windows, Azure, and identity systems. The post Beyond the benchmark: Advancing security at AI speed appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Taesoo Kim

item.title: Secret scanning updates – June 2026
item.description: Since our last pattern update, we’ve expanded secret scanning’s detection coverage with new partners, more patterns blocked by push protection by default, additional validity checks, and richer metadata for leaked… The post Secret scanning updates – June 2026 appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: ​​Forrester names Microsoft a Leader in the 2026 Extended Detection and Response Platforms Wave™ report
item.description: Microsoft has been named a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Extended Detection and Response Platforms, Q2 2026. The post ​​Forrester names Microsoft a Leader in the 2026 Extended Detection and Response Platforms Wave™ report appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rob Lefferts

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Migrate – GitHub Copilot Modernization integration for at scale code assessments
item.description: Azure Migrate now integrates with GitHub Copilot Modernization (public preview) to deliver at scale code insights. This capability brings together Azure Migrate’s portfolio-level discovery and assessment with GitHub Copilot’s context-aware code analysis.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks native read access to Microsoft OneLake
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports native read access to data stored in Microsoft OneLake through Unity Catalog. This capability is generally available. Customers can query and analyze data directly in OneLake without moving or copying data, enabling faster ac
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks natively storing data in Microsoft OneLake
item.description: Azure Databricks can now natively write managed Delta tables to Microsoft OneLake. This capability enables customers to use OneLake as a unified storage layer for Azure Databricks workloads without managing separate storage accounts. By storing data direc
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Intelligent sampling in Microsoft Foundry: the science behind selecting better production traces
item.description: Authors: Ilya Matiach, Morteza Ziyadi, José Santos, Ali Mahmoudzadeh, Shuo Qiu, Salma Elshafey, April Kwong, Vivek Bhadauria TL;DR Microsoft Foundry's intelligent sampling feature (used when creating an evaluation or fine-tuning dataset from production agent traces) uses a MinHash farthest-first...
item.dc:creator: imatiach

item.title: ICMP Support for Azure StandardV2 NAT Gateway
item.description: Our customers, across all industries, depend on consistent outbound connectivity and observability to operate and troubleshoot their cloud workloads at scale. As Azure environments grow in size and complexity, teams increasingly rely on standard network diagnostic tools such as ping to quickly...
item.dc:creator: malaikanazim

item.title: Enterprise-managed settings now support bypass permission controls
item.description: We’re adding our first governance capability to the enterprise-managed settings configuration. Enterprise administrators can now set disableBypassPermissionsMode to "disable" in the enterprise-managed settings.json to prevent GitHub Copilot CLI and VS… The post Enterprise-managed settings now...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: AI-Powered MSBuild Investigation with the Microsoft Binlog MCP Server
item.description: Diagnose MSBuild build failures and performance issues with AI using the new Microsoft Binlog MCP Server - 15 specialized tools that let your AI assistant investigate binary logs. The post AI-Powered MSBuild Investigation with the Microsoft Binlog MCP Server appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: .NET Team, Jan Krivanek

item.title: AI is accelerating cyberattacks—here’s how to stay ahead
item.description: See how Microsoft unifies identity and security signals to help teams prevent, detect, and respond to AI-accelerated attacks faster. The post AI is accelerating cyberattacks—here’s how to stay ahead appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sandeep Deo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: ICMP Support for Azure Standard V2 NAT Gateway
item.description: Azure StandardV2 NAT Gateway now supports outbound ICMP Echo Request and Echo Reply traffic, enabling customers to use tools such as ping to quickly validate outbound connectivity and troubleshoot network issues from workloads behind StandardV2 NAT Gatewa
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Auto mode in Copilot Chat available for all users
item.description: GitHub Copilot auto model selection is now generally available in Copilot Chat on github.com and the GitHub mobile app for all Copilot plans. With auto, Copilot chooses a model on… The post Auto mode in Copilot Chat available for all users appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Limit open pull requests for users without write access
item.description: Maintainers of open source repositories are dealing with an ever-growing volume of pull requests, including repeated low-quality or drive-by contributions that can slow triage and overwhelm review queues. To help… The post Limit open pull requests for users without write access appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Agent finder for GitHub Copilot now available
item.description: GitHub agent finder is now available. Instead of hand-wiring which MCP servers, skills, canvases, agents, and tools each agent should use—and filling your context window in the process—GitHub Copilot can… The post Agent finder for GitHub Copilot now available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Anatomy of the change
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl Incidents, alerts, correlation, and data—what actually changes with the new platform, and why it works in your favor. When you open Microsoft Sentinel in Microsoft Defender for the first time, the shift feels immediate:...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: Benchmarks in Microsoft Foundry (preview): Standardized model and agent quality checks
item.description: Authors: Ilya Matiach, Morteza Ziyadi, Han Che, José Santos, Ali Mahmoudzadeh, Shuo Qiu, Salma Elshafey, April Kwong, Vivek Bhadauria Introduction Benchmarks in Microsoft Foundry (preview) make that kind of measurement a first-class part of the development workflow. You can run well-known...
item.dc:creator: imatiach

item.title: Microsoft Options to Migrate SQL Server Databases
item.description: Overview This blog supplements the Migrate to Azure SQL documentation and tries to summarize the Microsoft first party ways to copy or migrate SQL Server databases from on‑premises environments to one of the flavours of SQL Server running in Azure. It is organized first by target platform (Azure...
item.dc:creator: Mitch_van_Huuksloot

item.title: Introducing New Additions to Microsoft Sentinel Normalization and ASIM
item.description: TL;DR: New ASIM parsers for Azure Firewall, Key Vault, AWS CloudTrail (EC2, S3, IAM), and 10+ third-party products. Two new schemas — Asset Entities and AI Agent Events. Plus changelogs on GitHub and a heads-up on an upcoming breaking change in ProcessEvent parsers. What's New Security teams deal...
item.dc:creator: derricklee

item.title: When your agent extensions fight each other
item.description: This is the fourth article in a series about Agent Experience (AX): the practice of making AI coding agents work correctly with your technology. The series covers what you can and can’t control in the agent stack, how to measure whether your extensions are helping or hurting, and how to iterate...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: Copilot individual plan sign-ups are reopening
item.description: We’re reopening sign-ups for GitHub Copilot Student, Pro, Pro+, and Max plans gradually over the next couple of weeks for new subscribers. If you’ve been waiting to get started with… The post Copilot individual plan sign-ups are reopening appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: v1.16.0-alpha20260617
item.description: 1.16.0-alpha20260617 (June 17, 2026) NEW FEATURES: Store PlannedPrivate data for providers (#37986) New store block in terraform_data that can handle ephemeral and sensitive values (#38298) Providers can now use nested blocks as computed values (#38305) import: add support for import blocks inside...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: How to Score a User Simulator: Introducing USR-8
item.description: Authors: José Santos, Shuo Qiu, Morteza Ziyadi User simulators have become a standard part of the agent-building toolkit: cheaper than real-user pilots, faster than scripted dialogues, and the only way to push an agent through hundreds of plausible conversations between every code change. The hard...
item.dc:creator: jcasantos

item.title: Azure Backup for Azure Cosmos DB Public Preview Adds Immutable Backups and Long-Term Retention
item.description: Azure Backup for Azure Cosmos DB Public Preview Adds Immutable Backups and Long-Term Retention Picture the first few hours after a serious data incident. A production application is down. Security teams are still trying to understand what happened. Application owners need to know which recovery...
item.dc:creator: Hans Wieser [MS], Jay Gordon

item.title: Introducing GitHub Pre-Purchase Plans: A Simpler Way to Plan Your GitHub Spend
item.description: As AI-powered development scales, more of what organizations pay for on GitHub is shifting from fixed per-seat pricing to usage-based billing. GitHub Copilot, Actions, Codespaces, AI Credits: spend now reflects what teams actually build, and that can vary month to month. For most engineering and...
item.dc:creator: kyleikeda

item.title: GitHub Copilot app generally available
item.description: The GitHub Copilot app is now generally available for macOS, Windows, and Linux. It’s the desktop home for agent-driven development, built natively on GitHub. Download the GitHub Copilot app to… The post GitHub Copilot app generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Build an Automated SLA Risk Agent with Routines in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: What if your support team could start every morning with an AI-generated summary of tickets most likely to breach SLA? This tutorial shows you how to build exactly that: a grounded agent in Microsoft Foundry that automatically analyzes your ticket data and surfaces risks. Using Routines in...
item.dc:creator: LauraVerghote

item.title: Azure Sets a New Performance Record for LLM Training Benchmark at Extreme Scale
item.description: Azure achieved the most performant MLPerf Training v6.0 result to date for Llama 3.1 405B, with a time-to-train of just over seven minutes according to MLCommons. This loadbearing benchmark measures how communication overhead and system stability dominate training performance, where Azure’s full...
item.dc:creator: azinheidarshenas

item.title: Closing the loop on container security: From code to runtime in the AI era
item.description: Containers are the backbone of modern cloud-native apps — and increasingly, the infrastructure powering AI, from AI assistants to a new wave of intelligent agents. They also blur the line between build, deploy, and runtime: a single code change can become a running workload in minutes. A...
item.dc:creator: mahersko

item.title: Azure Databricks at Databricks Data + AI Summit 2026: updates and new announcements
item.description: Databricks Data + AI Summit brings together the global data and AI community in San Francisco to share product news, technical breakthroughs, and customer stories. This year, as usual, we have a lot of Azure Databricks announcements, a strong presence across the event, and a continued focus on...
item.dc:creator: AnaviNahar

item.title: What are git worktrees, and why should I use them?
item.description: Git worktrees have been around since 2015, but it wasn't until recently they became popular. Learn what they are, how to use them, and why you might. The post What are git worktrees, and why should I use them? appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Guevara

item.title: Azure Arc Server May 2026 Forum
item.description: Please find the recording for the monthly Azure Arc Server Forum on YouTube! During the May 2026 Azure Arc Server Forum, we discussed: Private Preview of Windows Server 2016 Extended Security Updates (ESUs): Customers with Windows Server 2016 Servers with an NDA and willingness to provide feedback...
item.dc:creator: Aurnov_Chattopadhyay

item.title: (Co)work in Progress
item.description: A complete record of milestones, launches, and newest innovations. Delegate complex work​ – Copilot Cowork is now GA Now generally available worldwide, Copilot Cowork is an agentic system that plans, executes, and delivers real work. Getting to Cowork is now simpler. The redesigned Microsoft 365...
item.dc:creator: ilyagrebnov

item.title: GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers
item.description: We are retiring GitHub Models. As a first step, new customers can no longer use it. If your organization or enterprise have not previously used GitHub Models, you won’t see… The post GitHub Models is no longer available to new customers appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code
item.description: The Azure Functions extension for VS Code now provides a redesigned Create New Project experience in public preview, featuring a rich, visual template gallery that replaces the multi-step Quick Pick wizard with a searchable, filterable view of project tem
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Log Analytics Summary Rules experience
item.description: A new Azure portal experience for Summary Rules in Log Analytics is now available. Summary Rules enable aggregation of high-volume log data at a defined cadence and store it in summarized tables for improved query performance, reporting, or enhanced data
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing Public Preview: Agent Identities Asset Connector for Microsoft Sentinel
item.description: As organizations accelerate adoption of AI agents across Microsoft 365 and enterprise environments, security teams face a fundamental shift: Agents are becoming first-class identities and securing them requires understanding both their behavior and their identity context. We are excited to announce...
item.dc:creator: Krishna_Sagar_B_V

item.title: Aspire in VS Code: the 13.4 developer loop
item.description: Aspire 13.4 brings more of the local development loop into VS Code: AppHost CodeLens actions, live resource state, dashboard shortcuts, typed resource command prompts, TypeScript AppHost support, and multi-language debugging. The post Aspire in VS Code: the 13.4 developer loop appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Adam Ratzman

item.title: Transform your security operation with a unified experience in Defender
item.description: Co-authored with Lizet Pena, Caroline Mutua, Alvin Kua and Marco Sudahl Security operations teams today are being asked to do more than ever: respond faster, manage increasing data volumes, reduce operational complexity, stay ahead of evolving threats, and balance cost and efficiency. That’s why...
item.dc:creator: Mohit_Kumar1

item.title: Competing against yourself
item.description: You shipped a new CLI: better developer experience, modern architecture, and optimized for agents. You deprecated the old one, updated the docs, and blogged about it. Developers are migrating. Then someone asks an AI coding agent to scaffold a project, and the agent… uses the old tool. Training...
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: The Case for an Ontology Layer in Telecoms
item.description: The Challenge: Data Without Context in the Telco AI Era GenAI and analytics tools and solutions pull data from applications and from downstream and external data sources. However, when data is decontextualized from the applications where it was created, it often loses the business meaning,...
item.dc:creator: Alberto_Manuel
